entry.title: Here's a breakdown of the differences between the #SAP Cloud Connector and direct API integration
entry.content:
Here's a breakdown of the differences between the #SAP Cloud Connector and direct API integration:

SAP Cloud Connector

Purpose: The SAP Cloud Connector acts as a secure tunnel between your on-premise SAP systems and the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It allows cloud applications on BTP to access data and services in your on-premise landscape without opening up your firewall to inbound connections.


How it Works:
It's installed in your on-premise network.
It establishes an outbound connection to BTP.
It uses a reverse invoke proxy to allow BTP applications to call on-premise resources.
It provides fine-grained access control, allowing you to specify which on-premise systems and resources are accessible from the cloud.
Key Features:
Security: No inbound firewall rules required.
Simplified Connectivity: Easy configuration and management.
Centralized Access Control: Manage access to on-premise systems from a central location.
Protocol Support: Supports various protocols like RFC, HTTP, and TCP.

Direct API Integration

Purpose: Direct API integration involves establishing a direct connection between a cloud application and your on-premise SAP system using APIs (Application Programming Interfaces). This typically involves exposing APIs on your on-premise system and making them accessible over the internet.
How it Works:
APIs are exposed on your on-premise SAP system (e.g., using OData services or RFC-enabled function modules).
These APIs are made accessible via a gateway or API management platform.
Cloud applications consume these APIs using standard protocols like HTTP/HTTPS.
Key Features:
Real-time Integration: Enables real-time data exchange between cloud and on-premise systems.
Flexibility: Allows for more granular control over data exchange and application logic.
Standard Protocols: Uses widely adopted protocols, making it easier to integrate with various cloud applications.

When to Use Which?

SAP Cloud Connector: Best suited for scenarios where security is paramount, and you want to avoid opening up your firewall. It's ideal for extending on-premise functionality to the cloud and accessing on-premise data from cloud applications.
Direct API Integration: Best suited for scenarios requiring real-time integration, granular control over data exchange, and integration with third-party applications or mobile apps.

In many cases, organizations use a combination of both approaches depending on the specific integration requirements. The SAP Cloud Connector provides a secure foundation for hybrid landscapes, while direct API integration offers flexibility and real-time capabilities for specific use cases.

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_sap-activity-7280992374757965825-LKCg?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: SAP Integrated Planning usage in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

In my customer landscape SAP BW 7.5 on HANA DB used extensively for Integrated planning application using the BEx queries.

We plan to move on to SAP Analytics Cloud platform, we would like to utilize all the back-end developments in SAP GUI like Aggregation, planning sequences.

Is that possible from SAC?

Best Regards,

Vasanth

entry.title: Re: What is the file size limit when using the FTP adapter in SAP Integration Suite ?
entry.content: REMOVE COMMENT

entry.title: Re: What is the file size limit when using the FTP adapter in SAP Integration Suite ?
entry.content:
sftp to sftp - using streaming

there is 150 GB free space at the sftp server 

trying to transfer a 11 GB file we hit the following error 

Error Details org. apache. camel. StreamCacheException: Error during type conversion from type: org. apache. camel.component. file. remote. RemoteFile to the required type: org. apache. camel. StreamCache with value RemoteFile[dummy4.zip] due Error during type conversion from type: java. lang. String to the required type: org. apache. camel. StreamCache with value [Body is not logged] due java. io. IOException: error 28 - No space left on device, cause: java. io. IOException: error 28 - No space left on device

Conclusion - sap ci has some internal undocumented limit on the file size
The max size successully transferred 2.8 GB
If the 40 MB is the offically supported max size it seriously limits the usability of the product. It is the year 2025 and already 1995 most products supported file sizes above 40 MB. SAP should not be delivering a product which such a small limit in the year 2025.

Tested on suse openssh server and azure blob storage - the same problem on both servers.


see sap note
2648415 - Integration Flow Runtime Error "java.io.IOException: No space left on device"
no solution to the problem in the sap note except
create a ticket under BC-CP-IS-OPS to check the disk issue

A minimum requirement is that SAP has documented what is the limitation. Streaming implies unlimited file size but that is not the case.
It seems like the file transferred using streaming is actually stored at the CI tenant - if this is the case  then the file size is limited to the available storage space at the tenant - what is the available storage space at the tenant? Can the storage space be increased and how is the storage space increased?

entry.title: Configuring Email SCOT in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
The configuration of email sending in SAP S/4HANA is primarily done through the SCOT transaction (SAPconnect Administration). Here's a breakdown of the key steps involved:

1. Activate the necessary services:

  • Transaction SICF: Ensure that the SAPconnect service is active. You might need to configure the virtual SMTP server here as well.

2. Configure the SMTP node in SCOT:

  • Transaction SCOT: Navigate to Settings -> Outgoing Messages -> SMTP Nodes.
  • Create a new SMTP node or modify the existing one.
  • Provide the following details:
    • Node in use: Check this box to activate the node.
    • Mail host: Enter the hostname or IP address of your SMTP mail server.
    • Mail port: Specify the port number for SMTP (usually 25, 465 for SMTPS, or 587 for submission).
    • Address area: Define the allowed recipient domains (e.g., * for all domains, or specific domains like *.example.com).

3. Set the default domain:

  • In SCOT, go to Settings -> Domain.
  • Enter the default domain for your SAP system (e.g., yourcompany.com). This domain is used for sender addresses when users don't have an explicitly defined email address.

4. Maintain sender addresses:

  • Transaction SU01: For each user who needs to send emails, maintain their email address in the Address tab.

5. Configure send process (optional):

  • In SCOT, you can define how often emails should be sent (e.g., every 5 minutes). This is done by scheduling a background job for the SAPCONNECT program.

6. Test the configuration:

  • Use transaction SO01 or SBWP to create and send a test email.
  • Check the SCOT logs for any errors.

Important notes:

  • Authentication: If your SMTP server requires authentication, you'll need to configure this in SCOT as well.
  • Security: Consider using SMTPS (SMTP over SSL/TLS) for secure email transmission.
  • Troubleshooting: If you encounter issues, check the SCOT logs, the system logs (SM21), and the logs of your SMTP server.

Additional considerations for S/4HANA:

  • Cloud vs. On-Premise: The configuration might vary slightly depending on whether you're using SAP S/4HANA Cloud or On-Premise.
  • SAP Cloud Platform Integration: If you're integrating with other cloud services, you might need to use SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) for email sending.

This information should help you configure email sending in your SAP S/4HANA system. If you have further questions or need more specific guidance, feel free to ask!

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_scot-activity-7282289862005719040-XEb2?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: Pricing issue for split valuation MM
entry.content:
Dear SAP Expert,

We are facing a critical issue in our configuration and would greatly appreciate your assistance. Although we have identified the problem, we are unsure why it is occurring. Below are the details of our scenario:

Configuration Details:

  1. Split Valuation: We have implemented split valuation for material codes with the following setup:

    • Valuation Type 1 (External): Price control is set to "V" (Moving Average Price).
    • Valuation Type 2 (In-House): Price control is set to "S" (Standard Price).

  2. Scenario:

    • If we purchase a material, the batch is assigned to the External valuation type.
    • Both valuation types are used directly for purchase-to-sale or manufacturing within our plant.
    • In this case, after purchasing the material, we transfer it from own stock to a sales order stock using movement type 412 in MIGO.

Issue:

During the transfer, the system incorrectly considers the In-House price instead of the External price. As a result, the external price is inflated significantly. Upon reviewing CKM3, we noticed that the transfer material price is being recorded against the In-House price, which is not as expected.

Attached Screenshots:

For better understanding, we have included relevant screenshots illustrating the issue.

We would be immensely grateful for your guidance in resolving this issue.

Looking forward to your help!

entry.title: Re: Removing duplicate performance form records from Stor...
entry.content: I was experiencing the same issue when trying to create a story report on performance form status but did not know the cause until I read your question stating it was the collaborative step causing the row duplications. I used the count distinct solution on document id described in KBA 3092400 to implement a solution. Note: you need to change the document id from a measure to a dimension in the column options in the query first before creating the aggregation.

entry.title: BW/4HANA how to create a DTP filter interval with eclipse bw modelling tools?
entry.content:
Hello,

(sorry, screenshots are in german, I don't know how I can change it in eclispe; project with langauge EN doesn't help)

I want to change a DTP filter interval or create a new interval (and delete the old interval) in eclispe and I don't know how to do it. I think the existing interval was created before we did the BW/4 inplace conversion.

The field KDAY is from type DATS. I want to change the existing intervall "01.01.2019 - 31.12.2022" to "01.01.2020 - 31.12.2023". At the moment it looks like this.

If I try to insert "01.01.2020 - 31.12.2023" I get the message that the value is invalid

After "OK" the value is in the filter defintion, but the operator is = (equal). When I choose "Operator ändern" (change operator) the only possible value is = (equal).

I don't know how do to it in eclipse. (I want transaction RSA1 back)

Kind regards

Andreas

entry.title: GoBD / IDW PS880 Certification for SAP R/3
entry.content:
Dear all,

we are currently using SAP R/3 and we need a GoBD or IDW PS 880 Certification for it. Can somebody please help us?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: MRP run by MRP area
entry.content:
Hi Expert！

I want to run MRP on an MRP Controller basis set in the MRP area.

・Three MRP(Z1510, Z1511, Z1512) areas are assigned to material Z00001.
・MRP Controllers Z01, Z02, and Z03 are set in each MRP area.

When running MRP, I want to specify MRP Controllers Z01 and execute MRP.
In this case, I do not want to run MRP for MRP area Z1012 (MRP Controllers Z02).

Is it possible to achieve this?

 

Soma

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) - JDBC Adapter - Step-by-step guide - Pre-requisites and Configurati
entry.content:
Hello, I am struggling with inserting xml or even text with special characters into the PostgreSQL database through CPI JDBC adapter.

I set sql query via content modifier. But the iflow failed when the inserted text value contained a less sign followed by a slash ('</'). Inserting of text '<\/' works, but the query trully inserts all three characters into database, no escaping occurs.

I was trying this with a column of the Text datatype in PostgreSQL.

Have you faced this issue? Is there a way, how to escape the characters?

entry.title: Issue with Price Control in Split Valuation Configuration
entry.content:
Dear SAP Expert,

We are facing a critical issue in our configuration and would greatly appreciate your assistance. Although we have identified the problem, we are unsure why it is occurring. Below are the details of our scenario:

Configuration Details:

  1. Split Valuation: We have implemented split valuation for material codes with the following setup:
    • Valuation Type 1 (External): Price control is set to "V" (Moving Average Price).
    • Valuation Type 2 (In-House): Price control is set to "S" (Standard Price).
  2. Scenario:
    • If we purchase a material, the batch is assigned to the External valuation type.
    • Both valuation types are used directly for purchase-to-sale or manufacturing within our plant.
    • In this case, after purchasing the material, we transfer it from own stock to a sales order stock using movement type 412 in MIGO.

Issue:

During the transfer, the system incorrectly considers the In-House price instead of the External price. As a result, the external price is inflated significantly. Upon reviewing CKM3, we noticed that the transfer material price is being recorded against the In-House price, which is not as expected.

Attached Screenshots:

For better understanding, we have included relevant screenshots illustrating the issue.

We would be immensely grateful for your guidance in resolving this issue.

Looking forward to your help!

entry.title: PPF message not always processed, stucked in SPPFP (SMQS)
entry.content:
Hi guys,

we are a few days before the start of operations at the customer plant and we are encountering the problem that PPF actions are not always processed correctly. When the GI is posted in EWM, a PPF message is created, sometimes it passes it well (message processed and GI is posted in ERP), sometimes it gets stuck on SPPFP as a yellow mark and by manual execution it makes a statement as Time timit exceeded

In trsc SMQS it looks like this:

Target NONE should not be empty, but it is not:

We are encountering this problem on the quality system, I assume it came from many actions being run in a short period of time, but how is it possible that some of them are works and some are not? I also changed the "Max. Conn" value in SMQS for the target time from 10 to 12, but it had no effect...

I am are grateful for your advices

entry.title: Re: Mobile Printing via Bluetooth
entry.content: Can you please post link to the other post?

entry.title: Re: UI5 Building Blocks: How to enable global variant man...
entry.content:
Hi Junwu,

I clicked "Save As," entered the name "xyz" for the variant, and additionally checked the "Public" field. However, the variant is saved only locally.

Another user cannot see the variant "xyz." I checked the network traffic, and there is no call to save the variant. How can another user see the variant if it is not saved to the database? This is what I mean by saving it globally.

Thanks,
Jan

entry.title: Re: BW/4HANA how to create a DTP filter interval with ecl...
entry.content:
Hello,

I found the solution by myself.

I added first 20200101 and then 20231231. Then I marked it in the filter definition area and pressed the button "Bereich anlegen".

After that I had a second intervall 20200101 - 20231231. After leaving the window and activation of the DTP the interval is displayed in the german format.

Kind regards

Andreas

entry.title: Re: Relaxed Flow not working in Work Center POD
entry.content: Hi Nikhil, it really seems you are looking for "Any Order Groups" or even "Simultaneous Groups" in your Routing structure. Those would allow to deviate from the operation sequence, and in the case of Simultaneous Groups, even allow parallel processing of a single SFC in multiple operations at the same time. But, please be aware, that those structures are not dircetly supported by ERP or the ERP integration.

entry.title: Re: Unable to consume data from a odata service
entry.content:
Thanks to you I rechecked my destination and opted out the /$metadata from the /ZWEB_ORDERING_SRV/$metadata and now I am able to fetch the data.😅

Thank You @Dan_Wroblewski  for saving the day.😊

entry.title: Re: Initial entry
entry.content:
You want to manually post an initial entry of stock for a material in SAP S/4HANA. You want to enter both a quantity and an amount. How do you proceed? Note: There are 2 correct answers to this question.

A

In the SAP GUI, use transaction MIGO.

2

On the SAP Fiori launchpad, use the Post Goods Receipt without Reference app.

On the SAP Fiori launchpad, use the Manage Stock app.

com

On the SAP Fiori launchpad, use the Post Goods Movement app

entry.title: Custom Field is not visible in Manage Journal Entries despite published status
entry.content:
In the custom fields app we have created and published a field in relation to the business context "Accounting: Coding Block".

When checking this field in apps such as Manage Journal Entries I could not add the field to the available items of the Journal Entry.

It appears the custom field has not been published correctly?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Custom Field is not visible in Manage Journal Entries...
entry.content: It is possible that the field has not been enabled for usage in the app.  Please refer to KBA 3557962 - Custom Field (Accounting: Coding Block) is not visible in Manage Journal Entries

entry.title: Re: My favourite SAP Cloud Integration feature: JMS queues
entry.content:
Hi Morten,

We have a use case where we want to fetch JMS queues in a sequential manner, i.e. the first record that goes into the JMS queue should be the one processed first. Right now, what is happening is that all the JMS queue messages are processing in parallel, based on the number of worker nodes that are enabled. So, if we have 10 worker nodes it is processing 10 JMS queue messages parallelly, which is impacting some of our operations as some of the later queues are processing first. Is there any way by which we can control this behaviour? Is it possible to restrict the number of worker nodes to one for fetching messages from the JMS queue? Let me know if there is some possibility to overcome this challenge that we are facing.

Regards,

Priyank

entry.title: Export license check not required on batch split line items
entry.content:
Hello All

We have requirement, system should not check the export control license on batch split lines.  It should work  only at the header line item level.

FYI, we are not using the GTS functionality.  we are using SAP S/4HANA for International Trade.

Can one give some highlights how to switch off the export control license check for batch split line items in the delivery document.

entry.title: Re: Initial entry
entry.content:
For which of the following transactions do you receive error message if the automatic account determination is set up incorrectly? Note: There are answers to this question. 3 correct

Transfer posting between stock types

Stock transfer between company codes

 

Goods receipt into blocked stock

 

Purchasing info record price change

 

Material price change

entry.title: Re: SAP RAR posting with VAT / MWST
entry.content:
By default, tax condition category is excluded from transfer to RAR. Refer to the below note:

2610856 - FAQ: Condition handling in SD Integration Component - SAP for Me

You can use FARRIC_BADI_ORDER method INCLUDE_CONDITIONS to insert VAT condition types in the conditions table sent to SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting.

entry.title: Re: Can we reassign job requisitions automatically in SAP...
entry.content: Hi James,

Regards,

Tehreem

entry.title: Re: From Application Logging to Cloud Logging: Service Innovation Guide
entry.content:
For SAP Log Service, private cloud edition, that helps collect, centralize, store, forward, and access log files from all systems and applications that are in use. It centralizes the logs from customer’s systems, applications, and services running in a private cloud. Up to 50 servers are supported per tenant.

What's the tenant recommendation. In words how many tenant we propose to our new customer. Is their a way to determine it.

entry.title: Re: ABAP LLM Client Beta Release
entry.content: @Cocquerel I think the main difference is that ABAP AI SDK is SAP official software, while this ABAP LLM Client seems to be a community open source.

entry.title: Re: How to use the OData Client Proxy in SAP S/4 HANA or How to use the OData Service Consumption Mo
entry.content:
I followed this approach to generate the Outbound Delivery (A2X) consumption model in a BTP ABAP system and load the class into the newest available on-premise system - S/4 HANA 2023 FSP02 SAP Cal. I attempted using both version 2.0.0 & 2.1.0.

Both results contained syntax errors for missing interfaces that do not exist in on-premise S/4 HANA 2023 FSP02. Corrections to these missing interfaces are not addressed in the workaround replacement program zaf_r_create_mpc_from_scm20.

  • Class "/IWBEP/IF_V4_PM_FUNC_IMP" does not contain an interface "/IWBEP/IF_V4_PM_FUNC_IMP_V2".
  • Class "/IWBEP/IF_V4_PM_FUNCTION" does not contain an interface "/IWBEP/IF_V4_PM_FU_ADVANCED".

How should Business Accelerator Hub APIs such as Outbound Delivery (A2X) be used from ABAP on-premise client?

 

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

entry.title: Re: Seamless planning between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere is coming! Controlled release i
entry.content:
Where can I find more on the "same datacenter" requirement?

Suppose one has a an existing SAC running on AWS EU10 in Frankfurt and all other SAP BTP services are on Azure Amsterdam. Would there be a technical inconvinience e.g. related to latency or a technical impossibility to use seamless planning?

That would mean, in worst case, that a new SAC would have to be created and the contents of the EU10-operated SAC would have to be migrated to the Azure-based or vice versa, right?

entry.title: Re: Getting trouble when using TUNNEL Live Connection from SAC to SAP HANA on-premise
entry.content:
If you did everything according to procedure and it does not work, then it is difficult to address this via a community Q&A, unless @quovadis has some suggestions.

You might need some network or security experts from your organization to troubleshoot.

entry.title: asset inspection information use
entry.content:
hello,

i would like to know usage of "Asset Inspection Information Use" in equipment master.

entry.title: Supplier or Customer Master Data Upload using Migration Cockpit
entry.content:
To successfully create a Business Partner (BP) as a supplier or customer, it is essential to assign the appropriate BP Role and BP Grouping.

Business Partner Role:

The BP Role specifies the particular function a business partner will carry out within the system, whether as a supplier, customer, or contact person. Each role dictates the type of data that can be managed for the business partner and the processes in which they participate.

  • Supplier Role (FLVN00): Assigned to business partners who will function as suppliers in procurement and accounts payable processes.
  • Accounting Role (FLVN01): Relevant to suppliers engaged in financial accounting, including aspects such as payment terms and reconciliation accounts

Business Partner Grouping:

BP Grouping organizes business partners for more effective management. It categorizes them based on specific criteria such as relationship type. This categorization is crucial for efficiently managing business partners and ensuring they are linked to the correct processes and regions.

Let's proceed to create suppliers using the SAP Migration Cockpit.

Step 1: Navigate to App “Migrate Your Data – Migration Cockpit”

Step 2: Click create and select “Migrate Data using Staging Tables”

Mention the Name and click on Display Next Available

Chose Local Package and proceed for Step 3

Search Supplier and move to Selected Migration Objects Area.

Select the Supplier and click on Review.

You can check and verify the details and click on Create Project.

Step 3: Select your Project and click on Monitoring.

Navigate to Download Template -> Download XML File.

Open the downloaded xml file and fill the required supplier details

(Tabs with Yellow colour is mandatory to fill)

Fill Details in Company Code Sheet.

Fill the details in Purchasing Organisation Sheet.

Step 4: Upload the file

If upload status is green which indicates, file uploaded without any errors.

Step 5: Mapping Tasks

Click on Mapping Tasks.

Select Mapping Task and click on confirm, then click on Refresh Display.

All the tasks are confirmed.

Step 6: Prepare the Staging Tables

Click on Prepare and navigate to Monitoring.

Prepare Staging Tables Completed.

Step 7: Simulate and Monitoring

Click Simulate and navigate to Monitoring.

Simulation Completed.

 

Step 8: Migrating the Data

Click on Migrate and Navigate to Monitoring

Data Migration Completed.

Navigate one step back to see the Migration Results

Step 9: Check the supplier’s created using Migration Cockpit.

Check the suppliers list in “Display Suppliers” app.

Same you can check in “MKVZ-List of Suppliers”

entry.title: Re: BAPI_PR_CHANGE is not updating Service Component of PR
entry.content: Did someone find the solution for this?

entry.title: Re: What is the file size limit when using the FTP adapter in SAP Integration Suite ?
entry.content: Please see https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/inspect-top-integration-flows-by-storage-usage?locale=en-US&q=temporary+storage for some more information and you should see the 2 to 3 GB limit also here. Similar limits on our system

entry.title: Rounding Discrepancy for Goal Results Payout Amount – Variable Pay Management
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

 

I am currently working on a rounding / number format issue related with the field “Goal Results Payout Amount”.

Background of the issue: Due to rounding, the Business Payout Amount has a 0.01€ discrepancy to the Business Target Amount with 100% Target Achievement.

To solve this issue, we tried to increase the numbers of decimal places for the field “Goal Results Payout Amount”. Similar as suggested in KBA 2292772 - Business Section Amount and Target Payout Total have discrepancy - Variable Pay Management.

 

However, when increasing the number of decimal places for “Goal Results Payout Amount”, the precision of the field was not updated correctly:

Instead of displaying the correct value (13615.35 * 50% = 6807.675), the system uses the rounded value (6807.68) and adds the additional decimal places at the end.

I tried changing the number format multiple times (also via Provisioning), but without success.

Has anyone encountered this issue or knows if there are any specific configurations needed for the system to calculate as expected? Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your help!

 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: CPI | MPL Logs | Circuit Breaker mechanism
entry.content: Hopefully this guides you to find the most important iflows: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/inspect-monitoring-storage-usage?locale=en-US&q=temporary+storage

entry.title: Re: SAC Timestamp format change
entry.content:
Hi,

Have you tried using the description instead of the ID? With the correct language setting (English/English) it should work.

Regards,

J

entry.title: Re: Drop down with fixed values for a column in SAP CAP
entry.content: @SchiwekM Can you please elaborate specifically? As we are using SmartFilterBar with SmartTable in Fiori and translations doesn't seem to be working

entry.title: Re: Meet your new friend EMIS: Event Mesh in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
@AshishRaje The REST support should already be available. To be specific, it has been rolled out with Quality of Service 0 and for QoS 0 all associated APIs are available. Quality of Service 1 is planned for Q1/2025.

The NetWeaver Add-On for Event Enablement works as well with EMIS. There are already customers using it with EMIS.

Best,

Karsten

entry.title: Hi Comunity. I have an issue on an Idoc posting, Error Code KI235 - Account requires an assignment
entry.content:
I have an issue on an Idoc posting, Error Code KI235 - Account requires an assignment.

The path worked for one country but for other it stays into the 51 status with the error message KI235. same idocs do not have any field for cost center and no rule set into OKB9. I do not know where to look further to search what the issue could be on the France country where did not retrieve any CO object since on Spain worked with same logic on create Idoc.

entry.title: Software Component (Partner Demo Development Tenant) Multitenancy SaaS Application
entry.content:
Hello Dear Experts,

I am trying to create a software component to develop multitenant SaaS products in the partner demo development system. However, I get an error like the one below.

SCLM(Software Component Lifecycle Management) application is not configured in the current business tenant

 

 https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3530333

Resolution
TDD system (Partner Demo Development Tenant) is being used and in this tenant only predefined Software Components exist and other software components cannot be created:
 
  • Software Component ZPARTNER => Multi-Off Development
  • Software Component ZLOCAL => System Local Development which can not be transported or deployed


This is what SAP said about the error. In this case, we seem to be unable to create a software component, but is there a different way we can proceed? I wanted to ask you about something that can be done to create a software component.

Or do we need a different partner system so that we can create software components for our developments?

If we create a development package with the namespace we have previously registered in our partner development system and develop it with the ZPARTNER software component, will we have problems in the future when offering this product as a multitenant SaaS application? How should we proceed?

Reasons for Necessity:

Dear @FrankJentsch, your valuable comments and suggestions will make a great contribution. Thank you very much for your support.

entry.content:
Dear All,

Unable to find T4 related xmlschm file for 2024

Venkatesh

entry.title: Re: Getting trouble when using TUNNEL Live Connection from SAC to SAP HANA on-premise
entry.content:
just re-checked my test bed. the tunnel connection is working fine

Please make sure you can access your HANA DB via the Editor/Eclipse.
PS. my tunnel connection user

entry.title: saving a report using 2 queries on the same table (with different filters) using a merged dimension
entry.content:
when closing and reopening it i've seen two different behaviors :

- either the merged dimensions are "lost" and thus the data appear as "#SYNCDATA" until I remerge the dimension allowing to identify the lines I want matched in both requests and reaffect it to the details that depend on it

- either (more disturbing) it auto associates all dimensions that have the same name in both request ( all of them actually ... resulting in keeping only the data that are strictly identical, definitely not what I want)

using SAP BusinessObjects - Web Intelligence (WebI) SAP BusinessObjects BI 4.3 Support Pack 4 Correctif 6 with the new web interface and I didn't have that problem with the previous "old UI" version.

Use case behind : I have in the same table a price, fields allowing me to identify a specific line, and a beginning and end of validity dates. I want to recover in the same table both the current and previous price ... it works well with the merged dimension on the fields allowing to identify a specific line, but if I save and replay the reports, the merged dimensions get lost or unappropriately associated.

Is there a setting I miss somewhere that impacts the way merged dimesions are saved or "auto calculated" when a report is opened ?

entry.title: Re: B2B Composable Storefront Demo - Logins?
entry.content: Just now I tried to register and then i am successfully be able to login to storefront.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration - Creating XML Structure for Remote Function Call (RFC) that Requires Tables a
entry.content:
Hi Luis,
Thanks for the blog 
I'm trying to pass the the table as described but facing 500 Internal Server error.
Also when I'm not passing the OPTIONS Structure in the below body then it is giving 200 OK.

 

 

 

Body:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<ns1:ZRFC_AC_XXXXX xmlns:ns1="urn:sap-com:document:sap:rfc:functions">
    <QUERY_TABLE>BSEG</QUERY_TABLE>
    <OPTIONS>
        <TEXT>DATE >= '20050607' AND DATE <= '20100617'</TEXT>
    <OPTIONS>
</ns1:ZRFC_AC_XXXXX>

How can we resolve this?

Best Regards
Shivam

Thanks for detailing the functionlities of SDT using BTC.  Please confirm if we can carry out selective data migration based on time filter as well along with company code selection.

entry.title: Re: Excel Download with hyperlinks
entry.content:
Thanks Sandra & Raymond for your time and effort to solve this problem... Your inputs were of great value for me to proceed.
I turned to a CSV format file and converted to xml via fm: SCMS_STRING_TO_XSTRING. Though it wont have the formats that was needed, but at least I could get the hyperlinks on the file.

Thanks much for Your perusal and help!

entry.title: Re: In Public cloud getting error while updating the Juri...
entry.content:

Getting error message while updating Jurisdiction code in Company code address  " Jurisdiction code 0800301000 is not defined USA" This issue we are facing HANA Public cloud system.

Thank you 

JP

entry.title: Unable to POST Workforce Timesheet using OData Service
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I am working on a scenario where the requirement is to create timesheet of an employee in the SAP Public Cloud System. 

I referred to api.sap.com for the standard OData Service (https://api.sap.com/api/API_MANAGE_WORKFORCE_TIMESHEET/tryout)

As per the documentation i am posting this JSON payload : 

{
   "TimeSheetDataFields": {
      "ActivityType": "8",
      "WBSElement": "ADAPTIVE ROYALITY",
      "TimeSheetTaskType": "MISC",
      "TimeSheetNote": "THIS IS TEST NOTE",
      "RecordedHours": "9"
   },
   "TimeSheetDate": "2025-01-02T00:00:00",
   "TimeSheetIsReleasedOnSave": true
}

 But i am not able to see the response in Postman even if the response code is HTTP 200 OK

Please provide your valuable feedbacks. 


Best Regards, 
Prathmesh Sutar

entry.title: Sharing headers and exchange properties among parallel multicast branches in BTP IS (CPI)
entry.content:
Hello.

I have a scenario in which I need 2 branches from a parallel multicast, the first one have to control the execution time and the other executes the normal flow of the integration.

What I'm trying to accomplish doesn't work because each branch of the parallel multicast gets its own set of headers and exchange properties and modifying them in one branch doesn't take place in the other, also new headers and properties created in one branch are not present in the other. I don't know if this a bug or if it is intended, I guess my question is:

Is there a way to share headers and exchange properties among parallel multicast branches, without writing global variables or resorting to save data in data stores or JMS queues?

I tried changing the runtime configuration allowing headers and combining these allowed headers with all the possible options in HTTP Session Reuse, didn't work.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Consume External Rest API/Webservice Bydessing
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm hoping anyone is reading this.

Does anyone know a solution to this?

I am currently facing the same issue. Calling other resources on the same webservice with "POST" works and returns Result.Content but when using HTTPMethod GET it is always empty.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: B2B Integration Factory Q4/2024 Release highlights
entry.content:
Which requirements are crucial for your B2B business that you would like to see in the product?

Answer: Availability of OFTP2 Adapter capability.

entry.title: Re: Seamless planning between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere is coming! Controlled release i
entry.content:
Hi @MBode 

there is a useful support note here https://me.sap.com/notes/3515100/E with a section that states: 

Prerequisites apply:

  1. SAP Analytics Cloud running on HANA Cloud
  2. SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere are located in the same data center. SAP datacenters are not in scope
  3. SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere use the same identity provider
  4. SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere tenants are linked for seamless planning in a 1:1 relationship by the system owner

As such, we're encouraging customers to deploy datasphere (via BTP) to the same datacentre as their SAC instance. 

regards, H

entry.title: Re: Drop down with fixed values for a column in SAP CAP
entry.content: Hi Neetesh, it's actually not related to the Smart controls of FE v2. Rather CAP handles the translation based on the language parameter / header UI5 sends with the request. You can check out https://pages.github.tools.sap/cap/docs/guides/i18n#user-locale where the language determination process of CAP is described. Applying that should automatically translate the name property of the entity without the need to manually expand the texts navigation property. Hope that helps & BR, Marten

entry.title: SuccessFactors Joule - Joule and EC Quick Actions
entry.content:
Joule in combination with Employee Central provides us a huge set of use cases where Joule can support your employees and managers to run EC related processes. [List of transaction use cases]

To get this use cases running it is important to implement EC Quick Actions for the available cases. [Guide]

 

Let me try to show how the configuration of EC Data Model, Quick Action and Joule comes together.

1. Business Configuration: Here you define the fields, rules etc. that are available for employee data in overall and what you can see/change on the employee profile

2. RBP based on Business Configuration: Here you define who can see, edit, change, delete, create what details of whom

3. Configure Quick Action: With the configuration of the quick action you define what fields (from the datamodel/business configuration) are necessary for a dedicated process (e.g. if you have defined a process for working time changes than you can define that standard weekly hours, number of days per week are relevant for the process). The selected template defines the set of the available fields

4. Permissions for Quick Actions: Here you define who can run what quick action for whom (typically it is foreseen for Managers and Employees but it is also possible for HR)

With this 4 configuration steps you are ready to run EC based scenarios on Joule.

 

 

How can you validate if you can run a quick action based scenario on joule?

Let us assume I'm logged in as Carla and I want to change my own working location.

What do I need: 

- an EC Employee Profile (with bio info, job info, person info)

- permissions to run the "change location" quick action for me

 

To check the permission open the profile of Carla and check the available actions.

Because the quick action is available on profile level we should be able to run the scenario in joule also.

 

How does joule knows what information needs to be provided?

Joule is running step by step through the quick action definition.

You have defined what fields are relevant for the quick action. So Joule will ask you for each and every defined field if you want to change or to keep. 

If all the defined fields are provided user will see a summary and can save the data. In case a workflow is defined the workflow get's triggered.

One special hint: On the Quick Action template you can define what event reason is written for the defined action. In case you are NOT using event reason derivation you have to define the event reason on quick action template level.

 

Let us have a look to an example where the scenario on joule would not work.

Here we (again as Carla) see an "empty" profile where we have only an user but no employee data (no employment, no job).

In this case the quick action would not be available in the profile (even if my logged-in user has quick action permissions).

And so Joule will answer with "it is not possible".

 

 

 

 

Typical "issues" during testing:

- Quick Actions not defined

- No permission to run the quick action for the selected user

- selected user has no EC data (most common issue during testing on non-prod systems)

entry.title: Updating Short text of Inspection Lot using BO Interface - I_INSPECTIONLOTTP_2 (EML/RAP)
entry.content:
Hallo,

Link to details of Business Object Interface I_INSPECTIONLOTTP_2 - https://api.sap.com/bointerface/PCE_I_INSPECTIONLOTTP_2 

I am trying to use this BO interface to update the short text of Inspection Lot

 

DATA: ls_update_mapped TYPE RESPONSE FOR MAPPED I_InspectionLotTP_2,
      ls_update_failed TYPE RESPONSE FOR FAILED I_InspectionLotTP_2,
      ls_update_reported TYPE RESPONSE FOR REPORTED I_InspectionLotTP_2.

      MODIFY ENTITIES OF I_InspectionLotTP_2
  ENTITY InspectionLot
    UPDATE FIELDS ( InspectionLotText )
    WITH VALUE #( (
    %key-inspectionlot = <fs_insplot>-prueflos
                    %data-InspectionLotText = lv_shorttext
                    %control-InspectionLotText = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) )
    MAPPED ls_update_mapped
  FAILED ls_update_failed
  REPORTED ls_update_reported.

 

 

<fs_insplot>-prueflos has the inspection lot number and lv_shorttext has the new short text.

Problem faced is - ls_update_failed is always filled with error

CAUSE - UNSPECIFIC

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration – A Simple Demo Scenario Using the JDBC Adapter
entry.content: thank you for the detail.I am trying to achieve the same functionality but i want to receive email after every operation is performed. ex: after creation of table, insertion, deletion etc. I want to achieve it in one integration flow

entry.title: Assistance Needed with Substitution for FI Component (Event 0005)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

I encountered an issue while attempting to activate Cost of Sales (COS) accounting for a specific company. Below is the path I followed:

Path:
T.Code: SPRO > SAP Reference IMG > Financial Accounting > Financial Accounting Global Settings > Functional Area for Cost of Sales Accounting > Activate Cost of Sales Accounting

After selecting the desired company and marking "Active" in the COS column, I received the following message:
"Substitution for FI component, event 0005 not yet activated."

Could someone please clarify the following points:

  1. What is the "Substitution for FI component, event 0005"?
  2. In which scenarios is it used?
  3. How can I resolve this issue to proceed with activating COS accounting?

I’ve also attached an image for further clarification.

Best regards,
Mostafa Mahmoud

entry.title: Re: BADI J_1BNF_ADD_DATA - CHAMADA
entry.content:
Referente ao questionamento 1. 

SAP NOTE 2346434.

Ativando o fleg "Aut.Import.dds.adics" no local de negócio, automaticamente ao salvar a NF Writer ela irá acionar a BADI J_1BNF_ADD_DATA.

Att,

entry.title: Re: Assistance Needed with Substitution for FI Component...
entry.content:
Read first Deriving the Functional Area

  • Financial Accounting
    Global Settings -> Company Code -> Cost of Sales Accounting -> Set Up
    Substitution for Cost of Sales Accounting ->Prepare Cost of Sales Accounting (can be found in error message long text 😉)

entry.title: Re: Rebates (Condition Contracts) Partial Settlements with individual business volume
entry.content:
Hi @gvictor,

That's true. If we go with the final settlement for the Q4, it will follow the consolidated business volume method.

Regards

Hari

entry.title: Re: ABAP LLM Client Beta Release
entry.content:
I don't have any insights into the SAP ABAP AI SDK functionality but I assume it will focus on easy integration with their AI services and therefore give you access to many features. From the roadmap entry it seems it is targeted at SAP BTP ABAP envrionment first and more on the role of a developer assistant.

The community ABAP LLM Client tries to make it easy to call LLMs from within ABAP starting with 7.52 and without ABAP Cloud version support, so basically typical on-prem or other self-hosted environments. Probably a good option if you don't fullfill the prerequisites to use the SAP ABAP AI SDK and want to get started with this topic. I assume that with everyone going cloud the community ABAP LLM Client will be less relevant long-term. But yeah ... S/4HANA was first released nearly 10 years ago so long-term might be really long 😉

entry.title: Strategy maintenance plan re-scheduling in multiple maintenance package scenario
entry.content:
Hello, 

If in preventive maintenance strategy plan has selected multiple different maintenance packages for example 1 year and 5 year.

And if there is only one time exception and need to postpone only that 1year maintenance package +6 month, in situation that order is not called. How to call and re-schedule that 1 year packet +6 month, but not make any changes for 5 year package and calls

What is the best model for preventive maintenance strategy plan and what options and best practicies there is? Multiple maintenance items?

entry.title: Re: Master Data and Configuration Relationships in SAP S/...
entry.content:
Here some of the important points for your considerations.,

Financial Accounting :

OX15 - Company  

OX02 - Edit, copy, delete, check company code 

OX03 - Business Area

Controlling :

OX06 - Maintain controlling Area

Logistics General :

OX14 - Valuation Level 

OX10 - Edit ,copy , delete check plant

OVXB - Def, copy, delete check division.

Material Management :

OX09 - Storage Location 

Ox08 - Purchase Organization

Enterprise structure Assignment :

Financial Accounting :

OX16 - Company code to company

OX19 - Company code to controlling Area

Logistics General :

OX18 - Plant to Company code

OMJT - Business Area to plant 

Material Management :

OX01 - Purchase Organization to Company code

OX17 - Purchase Organization to Plant

Basic settings - Material Types

OMS2 - Define Attributes for Material types

MMNR - Material number ranges for material types

Plant Parameters :

OM18 - Carryout overall maintain of plant parameters 

OMD0 -Define MRP Controllers

OME4 - create purchasing Groups

OME5 - Define source list requirement at plant level.

OMWM - Define Valuation control

OMSK - Valuation class

OMWB - Configure Automatic posting

OBYC - Configure Automatic postings - Account assignments

Cheers

BR,

Sriram R

entry.title: how to get the curren row select by double click in Control Table
entry.content:
code 

      DATA: ws_field TYPE string,
            ws_line  TYPE string,
            repl     TYPE string.

      GET CURSOR FIELD ws_field LINE ws_line.

READ TABLE your_internal_table ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<workarea>) INDEX ws_line.
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
"implement your code...
endif.

Kind regards,

Sorina

Great update, thanks. Long awaited feature.

How do you handle the user authentification in SAP DM for a shared terminal scenario? One named user is being logged in, all others are only using their badge numbers?

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One 10.0 FP2208: Connection to the database is lost; contact your system administra
entry.content: I had to set the sysadmin role for the db user in order for it to work.

entry.title: STO creation with Quality/Blocked stock type
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement to allow intra/intercompany stock transfer from quality stock type. I understand this feature is available in SPM as described in KBA-3390111 but , I came accross another consulting note -3390689 in which it is mentioned that this process will work without errors only of the stock in quality stock type is not linked to an inspection lot for non EWM managed storage location.

My client belong to F&B industry and there it is quite common to have stock moved with the SC network in quality stock type for below reasons:-
1. Quality results usually take time and for that period the stock cannot be stored in factory due to space constraints.

2. in the DC to stock will in quality stock type which ensures this stock is not delivered to customer also stock in quality stock type is considered as available in stock for Supply planning purpose. 

Have you encountered such requirement from your clients and if yes, how have you addressed them.

 

Thanks,

Vinit.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM PPF Action read Condition record
entry.content: For any of the PPF actions tied to HU printing there should be one available already that will evaluate condition records and only initiate printing if the condition is met.

attaching screenshot for the same.

entry.title: Re: BADI J_1BNF_ADD_DATA - CHAMADA
entry.content: https://web.archive.org/web/20240116094840/https://blogs.sap.com/2015/02/12/utilizacao-de-ambas-as-badis-de-nf-e-j1bnfadddata-e-clnfeprint/

entry.title: Empty content in RESTCallResult for GET when using ExecuteRESTService
entry.content:
We have used several times

ExecuteRESTService and ExecuteRESTServiceWithoutEncoding. So far the RequestMethod had always been "POST" and everything worked fine. The body of the Response was always included in the returned RESTCallResult.content structure.

Now I want to add another call with one of the above methods in an absl script, but now the result is not returning any content in the absl file.

When calling the REST Service in Postman, everything works fine and I am sure that the webservice is returning the xml structure.

What I am missing here? What would be the correct way to get xml content from a GET REST Webservice if this is not the correct script method?

Michael

entry.title: Re: New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know
entry.content:
Hi Sanjay_Sota,

the implementation timeline can vary depending on the system complexity, customizations, and the existing setup. On average, it may take around three month, but some companies complete it much faster, while other may take longer.

Best regards,

Monika

entry.title: (Re)using Master Operations in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Introduction

By design, SAP Digital Manufacturing will always create order-specific objects like Operations, Routings and BOMs for every single new order downloaded from ERP. During this process, some fields are copied from master objects to the order-specific ones, see Enrichment of Order-Specific Objects for more details.

In this blog post, I will show you how to leverage "Operation (SAP ME)" and "Version" fields in the ERP Routing to create reusable master operation objects on the fly.

Standard Behavior

Out of the box, new order-specific objects are created for every single new order downloaded from ERP, like order-specific BOM and Routing:

And order-specific Operations:

 

As described at Enrichment of Order-Specific Objects, changes done locally in SAP Digital Manufacturing to master objects like Work Instruction and Data Collection assignments and other flags at BOM and Routing/Recipe levels are copied over and inherited from master to the newly created order-specific objects.

(Re)using Master Operations

As described in Routing Integration, "Operation activity master records can be created based on the SAP ME Operation and Version fields on the Operation record in the routing configuration.", which means that, at Routing level in ERP, "Operation (SAP ME)" and "Version" fields can be used to create and define master operations in SAP Digital Manufacturing.

 

Once defined in the routing, these fields are copied over to the production order in ERP.

And during Order Download process to SAP Digital Manufacturing, master operation objects are automatically created on the fly and reflect all over, see the examples below.

As master operations are now created and (re)used, you can perform settings and assignments directly against the master operations, like:

1 - Set 'Resource Type' directly at Master Operation level

2 - Assign other objects directly to the Master Operation, like Work Instructions, Data Collections, Buyoffs and etc

* As a remark, keep in mind that changes in Master Operations will affect every single orders using them.

Conclusion

Leveraging standard ERP fields and out of the box integrations, you can create and (re)use master operation activities in SAP Digital Manufacturing and perform settings and assignments against them.

 

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.

Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Change the IAS tenant integrated with Learning
entry.content:
We have an IAS tenant integrated with two Learning instances and the goal is to change one of the connected SF and Learning pairs to a different IAS instance.

According to offical SAP sources (3447162 - Change the IAS tenant integrated with Learning - Learning&IAS pairing - SAP for Me) it is not possible to re-run the IAS Upgrade for Learning but I am wondering if there is a workaround or a way to achieve this by manually performing the configuration (create IAS applications ,define endpoints, etc.)

Thanks for any help or ideas.

Kind Regards,

Andreas

entry.title: Re: When i save TEST script error shown "Test objects cannot be created in foreign namespaces&q
entry.content: Prefix your object name with Y or Z for instance (R3TR ECAT ZREMOVE_TCODE_FROM_ROLES). Don't use names of the SAP namespace.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI Keystore Expiry - Email Notification - Simplest of All
entry.content:
Hi chinmay,

Which roles are needed to get the keystore data? 

Thanks,

Vijay
